title: Food Systems Dashboard — Saudi Arabia Country Profile Export
abstract: Cross-cutting food systems indicators for Saudi Arabia exported from the Food Systems Dashboard, spanning food environments, food supply chains, individual diets and nutrition outcomes, drivers (biophysical, socio-economic, political, innovation), and policies, compiled from multiple underlying sources (FAO, NCD-RisC, Beal et al., World Bank, etc.) with an accompanying metadata file defining each indicator's sector, source, unit, definition, and calculation method.

resources.name: Food Systems Dashboard export — Saudi Arabia (CSV)

variables.name: Indicator
variables.label: Indicator Name
variables.description: Name of the food systems indicator (e.g. adult diabetes prevalence, availability of food-based dietary guidelines).

variables.name: Value
variables.label: Indicator Value
variables.description: Reported value for the indicator, country, and year range; unit varies by indicator and is defined in the companion metadata CSV.

variables.name: Name
variables.label: Indicator Name
variables.description: Indicator name, joinable to the Indicator column in the main export.

variables.name: Definition
variables.label: Indicator Definition
variables.description: Free-text definition of what the indicator measures.

variables.name: Source
variables.label: Underlying Data Source
variables.description: Originating source/study for the indicator (e.g. FAO, NCD-RisC, Beal et al. 2019).

steward.name: KAUST Human Nutrition and Health Data Team

access.how_to_request: Publicly available for export from the Food Systems Dashboard (foodsystemsdashboard.org); no request required.

provenance.originator: Food Systems Dashboard (Johns Hopkins University, GAIN, Columbia University)
provenance.license: Food Systems Dashboard open data terms (indicator-level source licenses may vary; see metadata file)
provenance.citation: Food Systems Dashboard. Saudi Arabia Country Profile. Accessed 2026-04-02.

known_issues: Compilation of indicators from many underlying sources with heterogeneous years, coverage, and update frequency; not a single coherent time series.
No sub-national spatial detail; national Saudi Arabia figures applied uniformly across all five subbasins.
Value column mixes numeric, percentage, and categorical (Yes/No) values depending on indicator; requires the metadata file to interpret correctly.
